windows.embed_text_v2: pouring molten yellow-green wax from a stainless steel pitcher to top off a large batch of silver metal candle tins, wooden wicks, then gloved hands straightening wicks, tiled counter
windows.summary_v2: A maker tops off a large batch of metal candle tins with molten yellow-green wax from a steel pouring pitcher, then adjusts the wicks by hand.

seconds_v2.desc: A large batch of silver metal candle tins packed on a white-tiled counter, each filled with glossy yellow-green wax and laid-across wooden wicks; a stainless steel pouring pitcher tips molten wax into tins at upper-left.
Wider angle over the grid of tins with a kitchen faucet at left; the steel pitcher now pours from the upper-right, topping off rows of half-filled tins.
The pitcher streams molten yellow wax into tins near the back-right; wicks stick up and lean across the glossy surfaces throughout the batch.
Pouring continues into the rear tins; the freshly filled tins in front hold smooth, reflective yellow-green wax to near the rim.
The pitcher tilts over another cluster of tins, raising their wax level; one front tin shows a bluish reflection on its liquid surface.
Steel pitcher moves along the back row, topping off more tins as the dense field of wicks and filled tins fills the frame.
Continued topping-off; the pitcher pours into tins at right while the bulk of the batch sits filled and glossy.
Final pour from the pitcher into the back-right tins, completing the fill across the tightly packed tray of tins.
Close-up cut: a person in blue nitrile gloves and a green-and-yellow plaid flannel reaches in with both hands to straighten and adjust the wicks in the just-poured tins.

windows.embed_text_v2: measuring and pouring yellow fragrance oil from a glass beaker into a tared stainless steel pouring pitcher on a digital scale, gloved hand, candle making counter
windows.summary_v2: A maker weighs bright yellow fragrance oil into a tared stainless steel pouring pitcher on a digital scale, pouring it from a glass beaker.

seconds_v2.desc: An empty stainless steel pouring pitcher sits centered on a digital kitchen scale reading 0.00 g (tared); more steel pitchers line the metal counter behind it, with a red heater indicator light glowing at left.
Same tared steel pitcher on the lit scale display; a blue-nitrile-gloved hand and a bottle of pale yellow fragrance oil begin entering at the lower-right edge of the frame.
The gloved hand at right holds a small glass beaker of yellow fragrance oil near the pitcher, preparing to pour; the scale still reads zero.
The glass measuring beaker of yellow oil is raised at right toward the empty pitcher's rim; surrounding steel pitchers reflect the studio light.
A clear glass beaker of bright yellow fragrance oil now sits prominently at the right edge, held ready beside the tared steel pitcher on the scale.
The beaker of yellow oil hovers at upper-right as the hand positions it over the open steel pitcher, scale display still at zero before the pour.
Yellow fragrance oil starts streaming from the tilted beaker at upper-right down into the empty steel pitcher; the scale begins to register weight.
The thin stream of yellow oil continues pouring from the beaker into the pitcher on the scale, a thermometer or wick rod standing inside the pitcher.
Pouring of yellow fragrance oil into the steel pitcher continues, oil bottle visible at right; the scale weighs the accumulating liquid for the recipe.

windows.summary_v2: A maker pours molten green wax from a stainless steel pitcher to top off a large tray of grey metal candle tins fitted with wooden wicks.

seconds_v2.desc: A tightly packed grid of grey metal candle tins fills a white tray on the counter, each filled with olive-green wax and holding upright wooden wicks; a stainless steel pouring pitcher tilts in at the upper-left.
Same batch of grey tins with glossy green wax and tall wooden wicks; the steel pitcher hovers at upper-left, poised over the back rows against a white subway-tile wall.
The steel pitcher dips lower toward the rear tins; front-row tins show smooth reflective olive wax filled near the rim, wicks leaning at angles.
Pitcher continues moving over the back tins to top them off; the dense field of grey tins and crisscrossing wooden wicks fills the frame.
The pitcher tilts further over the back-left tins; freshly poured green wax sits glossy in the foreground tins, some wicks bunched together.
Steel pitcher lowers near the center-back tins, about to release wax; the olive-green wax surfaces in front catch the light.
A clear stream of molten wax pours from the pitcher down into a back-center grey tin, raising its level as the surrounding wicks frame the pour.
The pour continues into the back tin, wax streaming steadily; foreground tins remain filled and reflective with their wooden wicks upright.
Molten wax keeps streaming from the tilted pitcher into the rear tin, topping it off within the packed tray of green-filled grey candle tins.

View tracks left along the rows of green-wax tins; wooden wicks splay at angles, surfaces of poured wax still glossy and liquid.
Front-row grey tins fill the frame, brimming with green wax; wooden wicks lean across them, more poured tins recede toward the tiled backsplash.

A metal pouring pitcher re-enters at upper left above the back tins, tipping toward them; foreground green-wax tins with wooden wicks remain still.
The metal pitcher hovers tilted over rear tins; foreground rows of green-filled grey tins with crisscrossed wooden wicks stay in focus, setting.
Pitcher still angled above the back rows; the full spread of grey tins filled with green wax and leaning wooden wicks rests on the white countertop.

seconds_v2.desc: Overhead view of tightly packed silver metal tins filled with amber-yellow wax, single cotton wicks draped across each rim; a glass beaker pours a clear wax stream into a tin.
The glass pouring beaker releases yellow-green melted wax into a tin near top center, surrounding silver tins already brimming with amber wax.
Beaker lifts and shifts left over the cluster of filled silver tins; cotton wicks lie draped over the rims, wax surfaces glossy.

Pour continues from the clear beaker into a silver tin; melted wax splashes level as neighboring tins sit full with draped cotton wicks.
Beaker held tilted, releasing wax into a tin top-center; overhead spread of silver tins brims with amber-yellow wax and laid-across wicks.
Glass beaker pours a steady wax stream into a tin near the top, the amber wax filling it as cotton wicks drape over surrounding rims.
Pour ongoing into a top-right tin; the dense overhead grid of silver tins glistens with freshly poured amber wax and crisscrossed wicks.
Beaker still tipped, finishing a wax pour into a top-right tin; the full overhead array of amber-filled silver tins with draped cotton wicks fills the frame.
